replication_study.hypothesis: The mean (signed-rank) difference between the average market price obtained by groups operating under the UNIFORM auction mechanism and the theoretical fixed price benchmark of 1.94 is greater than 0 (two–tailed Wilcoxon signed-rank test).

data_plan.dataset_identifier: fullsample_analysis.csv (plus auxiliary round1_raw.csv & round2_raw.csv preserved in replication_data)
data_plan.source_type: Laboratory-experiment log exported from an independent set of UNIFORM-mechanism sessions
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Groups from two independent experimental waves (Round 1 and Round 2 sessions) already collapsed to group-level means
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Experimental group (mean market price across 20 rounds)

qualification.explanation: The dataset originates from a fresh implementation of the same laboratory design and therefore constitutes an independent sample that can corroborate or challenge the original claim while preserving measurement fidelity.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Both data sets are derived from the identical IPO auction game described in the original paper and post_registration.json (laboratory experiment with 20 rounds, UNIFORM mechanism). Variables ‘price’, ‘dropout’, ‘bankrupt’ in fullsample_analysis.csv reproduce the exact measures used in replication_analysis.do that implements the Wilcoxon signed-rank tests documented in initial_details.txt.
qualification.deviation_from_original: Original study analysed 14 groups (post_registration.json → sample_size) collected years earlier; the replication_data hold 40 new groups created in two later experimental waves (round1_raw.csv & round2_raw.csv) which were not part of the original dataset.

data_plan.notes: Group-level prices were pre-computed in replication_analysis.do; raw participant-level logs remain available. Missing values occur only in rounds_missed_max (2 missing of 40) and are irrelevant for the focal test.

steps: For each group compute the signed difference price − 1.94 (benchmark).
Run Wilcoxon signed-rank test on all groups.
Repeat test excluding groups flagged dropout==1, and excluding both dropout==1 or bankrupt==1 (robustness checks).
Save results to JSON and print to console.
planned_method.independent_variables: Constant benchmark value 1.94; no varying covariate because hypothesis is about deviation from fixed price
planned_method.control_variables: Filters: dropout indicator, bankrupt indicator (used only to create robustness subsets, not included in the statistic)

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Wilcoxon signed-rank statistic T comparing paired differences to zero

planned_method.missing_data_handling: List-wise deletion (drop NaN in price column)
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: None (single focal outcome; additional filtered tests reported as robustness without adjustment)
planned_method.inference_criteria: Reject H0 if p < 0.05 (two-tailed) and median price > 1.94 to match directional expectation

files.replication_analysis__py.py: Reads fullsample_analysis.csv, performs Wilcoxon signed-rank tests (all groups, no-dropout subset, no-dropout-no-bankrupt subset), prints results, and saves them to /app/data/replication_results.json

codebase.notes: File is a faithful Python translation of replication_analysis.do; all paths changed to /app/data. The script is self-contained and uses only open-source libraries.

analysis.comparison_metrics: Compare replication p-value and direction (median price relative to 1.94) against original p = 0.001 and positive direction as reported in initial_details.txt / post_registration.json.
